title: CCRG iter-9 Positioning: Localization Commit, Label-Efficiency Both-Forks, Cite Lock
layman_summary: Frames an AI-interpretability paper so it reads well whichever way a pending experiment lands, and gathers plus fact-checks every supporting citation for both outcomes.
summary: Pure web-research positioning ($0, no code) for iteration-9 of the CCRG paper, securing the framing for GEN_PAPER_TEXT before the make-or-break label-scarce experiment (M1'''') resolves in a parallel artifact; every result-dependent passage is supplied in BOTH fork variants plus a selector. BLOCK 1 (S1) COMMITS the reposition away from 'clustering' to label-free single-specialist LOCALIZATION ('recall-hole-anchored absorber localization'), reporting the multi-member clustering hypothesis as a TESTED-AND-NEGATIVE finding (set-cover inert 0/8 edit cases, 3/8 same latent; multi-member adds collateral; all three goal-named downstream tasks — safety classification, steering, model-diffing — are nulls) backed by the literature that predicts it (DeepMind negative-results/deprioritisation blog, AxBench, Arad/Mueller/Belinkov, SAEs-not-canonical, Peng discover-not-act), with homograph-confinement as the deliberate HEADLINE; drop-in abstract spine + intro opener + 5 retitle options (recommended: 'Where Absorption Hides …'). BLOCK 2 (S2) GROUNDS the label-scarce where-to-gate demonstration in the label-efficiency / few-shot-probing / active-learning-for-probing / steering-data-efficiency literature (Whitney loss-data curve for the empirical quality-vs-#labels methodology — explicitly NOT a sample-complexity theorem; Im&Li diff-of-means-optimal and Tan/Chanin steering-vector-reliability for the supervised gate's label cost; Rauch No-Free-Lunch AL over frozen LLM embeddings + PEAL[vision]/PALP for active-learning-reduces-but-needs-labels; Peng/label-free-SAE for zero-label discovery), writing BOTH forks (FORK-A demonstrated where-to-gate value = saves the per-sub-context labeling cost; FORK-B fair gate matches at n=1 = clean localization+confinement boundary paper) + the selector (FORK-A iff CI separation at n in {1,5} on edit OR localization; n=0 trivial; iter-8 prior favors FORK-B / localization-axis FORK-A). BLOCK 3 (S3) articulates the lexical-polysemy confinement 'so what' + the shipped $0 label-free practitioner SCREEN against the SAE-reliability/auditing literature (Chanin 'Are SAE Benchmarks Reliable?' — proxy metrics fail/don't transfer; Minegishi polysemous-words eval; Gallifant SAE-features-transfer for the auditing use-case; Ahsan&Wallace co-firing corroboration) + the coverage-result framing. BLOCK 4 (S4/S5) carries the iter-6/7/8 venue-locked citation table VERBATIM, adds 10 web-verified new cites with exact IDs/venues/authors + BibTeX + unresolved flags (notable: David Chanin authored 3 reliability cites), and supplies a 13-item presentation-strip checklist extending iter-8's list with the iter-9 R4 (selectivity-as-localization not surgical-advantage; whole-parent strawman dropped; KG collateral 5.1e-5 vs fair gated dense 2.8e-6), R5 (stronger non-eval-aligned repair control + downstream-capability test or temper to localization), and R6 (strip iteration/rebuttal scaffolding) fixes, plus the carried spine numbers (1452x/1262x, 722x/676x, 65k FULL/layer-9 PARTIAL, 2/44, member-labeling 0.730 vs 0.096, model-diffing +0.000) and the defensible lead +1.00 CI[0.79,1.21] not +1.58. Outputs research_out.json + research_report.md (S1-S5).
answer:
This iteration-9 positioning pass finalizes the CCRG paper framing for GEN_PAPER_TEXT so the draft is coherent WHICHEVER way the make-or-break label-scarce experiment (M1'''') lands — M1'''' runs in a PARALLEL artifact and its verdict is UNKNOWN at research time, so every result-dependent passage is supplied in BOTH fork variants plus a selector, exactly as iter-8 supplied OUTCOME-A/B. Four blocks (full drop-in prose, tables, BibTeX, and the checklist are in research_report.md S1–S5).

=== BLOCK 1 (S1): COMMIT THE LOCALIZATION REPOSITION; report the clustering null as a FINDING (M2''''/R1) ===
The central object is renamed ONCE to 'recall-hole-anchored absorber localization' — a training-free, label-free procedure that surfaces the single absorber latent marginal-attribution selection silently drops; 'CCRG/clustering/grouping' survives only as a legacy changelog name and must leave the title/abstract/section leads. The abstract spine and intro opener (S1.1/S1.2) STATE UP FRONT that the multi-member CLUSTERING hypothesis (the goal's own ask) was tested and did NOT pay off: the set-cover/multi-member machinery is inert against a max-precision single-latent selector (adds value in 0/8 edit cases, returns the identical latent in 3/8), multi-member units only ADD collateral, and all THREE goal-named downstream tasks are nulls (no SAE unit out-classifies a dense probe; steering surgical on only 2/5 first-letter cases; model-diffing confound-bounded +0.000). This null is framed as a deliberate, reportable finding backed by the literature that PREDICTS it: the Google DeepMind Mechanistic Interpretability Team's 'Negative Results for Sparse Autoencoders … and Deprioritising SAE Research' (Mar 26 2025) reports dense linear probes 'perform nearly perfectly, including out of distribution' while k-sparse SAE probes are 'distinctly worse … on the OOD set', and the team pivots to 'model diffing, interpreting model organisms of deception, and … thinking models' [1] — the single best anchor because it predicts the probing/classification null AND independently flags model-diffing (our +0.000 task) as the hard open direction; AxBench [12] and Arad/Mueller/Belinkov [16] predict the steering null; 'SAEs Do Not Find Canonical Units' [14] motivates why the question was worth asking; Peng 'discover-not-act' [11] gives the constructive frame. Homograph-confinement (absorption is lexical-polysemy: 2/44 safety groups both homographs, 0/28 professions, 3/64 entities) is the deliberate HEADLINE scientific contribution. Five retitle options that drop 'clustering/grouping' and commit to localization + screen are supplied (S1.5); the recommended title is 'Where Absorption Hides: Training-Free, Label-Free Localization of Homograph-Polysemy Feature Absorption in Frozen SAEs — and a Screen to Find It'. Target ICLR primary (CfP fit: fairness/safety/privacy + interpretation of learned representations [22]), ICML fallback.

=== BLOCK 2 (S2): GROUND THE LABEL-SCARCE WHERE-TO-GATE DEMONSTRATION — BOTH FORKS (M1''''/R2) ===
M1'''' varies n∈{0,1,5,20,full} sub-context labels available to the genuinely-FAIR conditional-dense gate (which needs labels for BOTH the direction u_sub(n) AND the detector d_sub(n)) and compares, at each n, against the label-free SAE absorber handle (KG-ABL, gated by the absorber's own JumpReLU firing; zero sub-context labels) on EDIT quality and LOCALIZATION (gate balanced-accuracy) quality; the SAE line is flat in n, the dense line rises with n, and the decisive signal is low-n CI separation. METHODOLOGY grounding: frame the curves as an EMPIRICAL label-efficiency evaluation using the loss-data-curve / ε-sample-complexity vocabulary of Whitney et al. [2], and EXPLICITLY disclaim any sample-complexity THEOREM (the goal forbids CL-theory contributions). LABELING-COST grounding (the FORK-A numerator): the strongest fair dense comparator is exactly the difference-of-means direction, which Im & Li prove optimal/strongest and which is built from LABELED contrast pairs [3]; Tan/Chanin et al. (NeurIPS 2024) show steering directions are dataset-dependent, variable, and brittle, so the supervised gate's quality degrades with its label budget [4]; active-learning-for-probing can REDUCE but never remove the label cost — Rauch et al. benchmark deep AL over frozen LLM embeddings and find gains are query-strategy- and embedding-quality-sensitive (LLM-domain anchor) [5], with PEAL (CVPR 2024 WS; VISION-modality FLAG) [6] and PALP (AAAI 2023) [7] as the active-learning/few-shot-probing backdrop — whereas the SAE route needs ZERO sub-context labels [11, 21]. BOTH forks are written as drop-in paragraphs + abstract slots (S2.3/S2.4): FORK-A 'DEMONSTRATED_WHERE_TO_GATE_VALUE' = the SAE handle holds quality where the fair gate collapses at low n (break-even n_be=X), so label-free discovery SAVES the ~X-label-per-sub-context cost the strongest fair supervised alternative incurs — the concrete SAE-specific positive and the explicit DELTA over the SUPERVISED gated-steering prior art (CAST [19]/GSS [20]); FORK-B 'FAIR_GATE_MATCHES_AT_N1' = the fair gate matches even at n=1, so there is NO SAE-specific where-to-gate value, the edit is a scoped capability over an unconditional dense projection only, and the paper retargets fully to label-free LOCALIZATION + the confinement SCREEN (an honest, publishable boundary paper). SELECTOR (S2.5): use FORK-A iff CI separation favors the SAE handle at n∈{1} (ideally also n=5) on edit OR localization quality; n=0 is trivial (dense undefined) and must NOT trigger FORK-A; per the iter-8 prior (fair gate already MATCHED at full labels: BEATS=0/FAIR_CLOSES=4/n_concentrated_wins=0), the realistic expectation is FORK-B, or a FORK-A that lands on the LOCALIZATION (fit-free absorber gate vs low-n d_sub gate) axis rather than edit quality. FORK-B does not sink the paper.

=== BLOCK 3 (S3): CONFINEMENT 'SO WHAT' + SHIPPED LABEL-FREE SCREEN (M3''''/R3) ===
The 'why others build on this' argument is positioned against the SAE-reliability/auditing literature. (i) The finding: SAE feature-absorption reliability is a LEXICAL-POLYSEMY phenomenon (first-letter spelling + homograph entities), NOT a demographic/semantic one — 2/44 safety attributes (white/straight, both homographs), 0/28 professions, 3/64 entities; the rest are CO-FIRING/entangled (corroborated by Ahsan & Wallace's single race latent firing jointly on the attribute and 'incarceration', of 'marginal utility for realistic tasks' [17]); this polysemy-governed reliability is exactly what Minegishi et al.'s polysemous-words SAE evaluation predicts [9]. So practitioners auditing SAFETY attributes on a frozen SAE need not fear absorption there. (ii) The deliverable: a $0 label-free SCREEN (recall-hole + firing-disjoint + precision, validated non-circularly against the form-free probe-plus-ablation oracle on a stratified sample) that runs on ANY frozen public SAE (Gemma Scope, Neuronpedia, single GPU) with NO diagnostic probe and NO sub-context labels, returning absorption-structured yes/no + the NAMED hole + screen-vs-oracle agreement. (iii) Why build on it: the field's OWN SAE-reliability benchmarks are unreliable and do not transfer — Chanin's 'Are Sparse Autoencoder Benchmarks Reliable?' finds TPP and SCR 'fail multiple lenses … should not be used', even the best proxy (sae-probes) cannot separate SAE variants, and 'the field needs better SAE benchmarks' [8] — so a task-grounded, label-free, oracle-validated screen is a concrete transferable reliability instrument, complementary to the auditing/transfer role SAE features already play (smaller-model SAE features transfer to larger models, Gemma 2 2B→9B, macro-F1>0.8 over hidden-state/BoW; cite for the auditing use-case, NOT as a beats-dense claim) [10], and answering the negative-results literature's call for better interpretability tools [1]. M3'''' reports a single COVERAGE number (fraction of N≥200–300 mined candidate polysemous tokens that are absorption-structured, Wilson/bootstrap CIs, stratified by hierarchy) with positive (Georgia/large/Amazon/months) and negative (professions/most cities) controls; if it did not return by paper time, fall back to the locked breadth counts framed as a partial coverage table.

=== BLOCK 4 (S4/S5): CITES + PRESENTATION STRIP (M6'''') ===
The iter-6/7/8 venue-verified citation table is carried VERBATIM (Chanin absorption 2409.14507 NeurIPS2025; AxBench/SAEBench/Matryoshka ICML2025; SAEs-not-canonical 2502.04878 ICLR2025; CAST 2409.05907 ICLR2025-Spotlight; GSS 2602.08901; SADI ICLR2025; Arad/Mueller/Belinkov EMNLP2025; CorrSteer/FGAA/SAS/Duan/SAE-TS; Scaling Monosemanticity; the full safety/fairness/unlearning set; Peng 2506.23845; label-free SAE 2506.01247; LEACE; JTT/GEORGE/EIIL/LfF). TEN new cites were web-verified this iteration with exact IDs/venues/authors (S4.2): DeepMind negative-results blog (Mar 2025) [1]; Whitney et al. 2009.07368 (preprint; loss-data curve) [2]; Im & Li 2502.02716 (preprint; unified steering, diff-of-means optimal) [3]; Tan/Chanin et al. 2407.12404 (NeurIPS 2024; steering-vector reliability) [4]; Rauch et al. 2506.01992 (preprint; No-Free-Lunch active learning over frozen LLM embeddings) [5]; Lakshmi Narayanan et al. 2406.09296 (CVPR 2024 WS; PEAL — VISION FLAG) [6]; Cho et al. 2212.10873 (AAAI 2023; PALP) [7]; Chanin 2605.18229 (preprint; Are SAE Benchmarks Reliable?, sole author) [8]; Minegishi et al. 2501.06254 (ICLR 2025; polysemous-words SAE eval) [9]; Gallifant et al. 2502.11367 (EMNLP 2025; SAE features for classification/transfer) [10]. Notable through-line: David Chanin authored/ co-authored three of the load-bearing reliability cites ([4], [8], and the carried absorption diagnostic [15]). BibTeX for all ten and an unresolved-flags list are in S4.3/S4.4 (verify Rauch author count; PEAL is vision-only; [2]/[3]/[8] are preprints; carried CorrSteer/DeBiasLens/Karvonen-Marks/SAEBench venue flags). The 13-item presentation-strip checklist (S5) extends iter-8's list with the iter-9 fixes: ONE stable name + appendix operator table; strip iteration/rebuttal scaffolding (R6); selectivity-as-LOCALIZATION not surgical-advantage with the fair-gate cross-reference (KG collateral ≈5.1e-5 vs fair gated dense ≈2.8e-6) and the whole-parent strawman DROPPED (R4); stronger non-eval-aligned repair control + downstream-capability test or temper to 'localization not utility' (R5); lead edit = KG vs strongest UNGATED dense = +1.00 CI[0.79,1.21] on 'large' (never +1.58-vs-footprint-gated); both forget instruments; concentration not absorption; localization-not-classification; homograph-confinement headline; carry settled spine numbers (1452×/1262×; 722×/676×; 65k FULL/layer-9 PARTIAL; member-labeling 0.730 vs 0.096; model-diffing +0.000); and drop in the FORK-A/B passage per the selector.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH on all ten new cite IDs/authors/venues (grep-verified from arXiv abstract pages and ACL Anthology), on the DeepMind negative-results content, and on the carried-table contents (read directly from the three dependency research_report.md files). MEDIUM only on the carried inherited venue flags (CorrSteer ICML-vs-ICLR; SAEBench reconfirm; Rauch 6th-author check), all explicitly flagged for camera-ready. The single largest uncertainty is exogenous: the M1'''' verdict, which is why both forks + a selector are mandatory.

sources.title: Negative Results for Sparse Autoencoders On Downstream Tasks and Deprioritising SAE Research (DeepMind Mechanistic Interpretability Team)
sources.summary: Mar 26 2025 progress update. Dense linear probes 'perform nearly perfectly, including out of distribution'; k-sparse SAE probes 'distinctly worse … on the OOD set'; team DEPRIORITISES SAE research and pivots to 'model diffing, interpreting model organisms of deception, and … thinking models'; cites Kantamneni/Farrell/Arora (negatives). The Block-1 anchor that PREDICTS the classification/probing null AND independently flags model-diffing (our +0.000 task) as the hard open direction.

sources.title: Evaluating representations by the complexity of learning low-loss predictors (Whitney, Song, Brandfonbrener, Altosaar, Cho)
sources.summary: Introduces the loss-data curve, Surplus Description Length (SDL), and ε-sample-complexity for measuring how much labeled data a representation needs to hit a target loss. Grounds the M1'''' quality-vs-#labels CURVE methodology and the 'empirical label budget / break-even n' vocabulary — explicitly an empirical measure, NOT a theorem (respects the goal's avoid-CL-theory constraint). Preprint (2020, v2 2021; OpenReview FOR2VqgJXb; no journal-ref).

sources.title: A Unified Understanding and Evaluation of Steering Methods (Im & Li)
sources.summary: Mean-of-differences (difference-of-means) is the provably-optimal and empirically strongest steering vector vs PCA/classifier alternatives. Justifies u_sub=diff-of-means(target-positive, sibling-positive) as the strongest FAIR dense comparator AND establishes that building it needs per-sub-context LABELED contrast pairs — the FORK-A numerator. Preprint (cs.LG/cs.CL; v2 Jan 2026; no venue in Comments).

sources.title: Analysing the Generalisation and Reliability of Steering Vectors (Tan, Chanin, Lynch, Paige, Kanoulas, Garriga-Alonso, Kirk)
sources.summary: NeurIPS 2024. Steerability is 'mostly a property of the dataset rather than the model', highly variable across inputs, brittle OOD — so a supervised steering/gate direction is not a free lunch and its quality depends on the labeled set it is fit from; supports 'the dense gate degrades when its label budget is small' (FORK-A axis). David Chanin co-author (reliability through-line with [8],[15]).

sources.title: No Free Lunch in Active Learning: LLM Embedding Quality Dictates Query Strategy Success (Rauch, Wirth, Huseljic, Herde, Sick)
sources.summary: Benchmarks deep active learning over FROZEN LLM embeddings on 10 text-classification tasks; optimal query strategy is sensitive to embedding quality — AL REDUCES but never removes the label requirement and its gains are not guaranteed. The LLM-domain anchor for 'the labeled gate's cost can be reduced by active learning, yet still needs labels; the label-free SAE route needs none'. Preprint (18 May 2025); verify 6th author.

sources.title: Parameter-Efficient Active Learning for Foundational models (Lakshmi Narayanan, Krishnan, Subedar)
sources.summary: CVPR 2024 Transformers-for-Vision Workshop (cs.CV — VISION modality, FLAG). AL + LoRA over frozen-feature linear probes in budget-constrained classification. Secondary active-learning anchor; cite ONLY for the active-learning-reduces-but-needs-labels principle, never as same-domain (text/LLM) evidence (use [5] for that).

sources.title: Prompt-Augmented Linear Probing: Scaling beyond the Limit of Few-shot In-Context Learners (Cho, Kim, Kim, Kim, Lee, Yoo)
sources.summary: AAAI 2023. Hybrid of linear probing and in-context learning that inherits linear probing's scalability in labeled samples. Secondary few-shot-linear-probing label-efficiency backdrop for Block 2.

sources.title: Are Sparse Autoencoder Benchmarks Reliable? (Chanin)
sources.summary: Preprint (18 May 2026), sole author David Chanin. Audits SAEBench via three lenses; TPP and SCR 'fail multiple lenses … should not be used to evaluate SAEs'; other metrics noisier/less discriminative than assumed; even sae-probes cannot separate variants of the same SAE architecture; 'the field needs better SAE benchmarks'. The load-bearing R3 anchor: the field's own proxy reliability metrics are unreliable/non-transferable, so a task-grounded label-free oracle-validated screen fills a real gap.

sources.title: Rethinking Evaluation of Sparse Autoencoders through the Representation of Polysemous Words (Minegishi, Furuta, Iwasawa, Matsuo)
sources.summary: ICLR 2025 ('Published at ICLR2025'). Evaluates SAEs via polysemous words; MSE-L0-Pareto improvements can CONFUSE interpretability rather than improve monosemantic extraction. Directly grounds the LEXICAL-POLYSEMY framing of the confinement finding — SAE absorption/reliability is fundamentally a polysemy phenomenon, so confinement to homograph/spelling polysemy is the expected shape.

sources.title: Sparse Autoencoder Features for Classifications and Transferability (Gallifant, Chen, Sasse, Aerts, Hartvigsen, Bitterman)
sources.summary: EMNLP 2025 (2025.emnlp-main.1521; arXiv 2502.11367). SAE-derived features reach macro-F1>0.8 on safety-critical classification and transfer from a smaller model to a larger one (Gemma 2 2B→9B-IT) and cross-lingually. Cite for the auditing/oversight use-case the screen serves (smaller-model SAE structure predicts larger-model behaviour); note the nuance — it beats hidden-state/BoW under POOLING, which does not contradict our localization-not-classification (no single-latent out-classifies-dense) claim.

sources.title: Use Sparse Autoencoders to Discover Unknown Concepts, Not to Act on Known Concepts (Peng, Movva, Kleinberg, Pierson, Garg)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked). Verbatim thesis: SAEs are 'powerful tools for discovering unknown concepts' but 'less effective for acting on known concepts'. The constructive spine for the reposition — discovery/localization is the surviving positive; the FORK-B 'discover-not-act' frame; the label-free-discovery-is-the-value spine of FORK-A.

sources.title: AxBench: Steering LLMs (Wu et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, ICML 2025). Simple baselines (difference-of-means) beat SAEs on detection and beat SAE steering on aggregate. Predicts the steering null and grounds localization-not-classification; the side-effect/fluency LLM-judge eval bar the edit arm borrows.

sources.title: SAEBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Sparse Autoencoders (Karvonen et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, ICML 2025). The field's SAE evaluation suite (includes feature-absorption + sparse-probing metrics) that Chanin [8] audits and finds unreliable. Reconfirm ICML-vs-ICLR at bib-time (OpenReview qrU3yNfX0d exists).

sources.title: SAEs Do Not Find Canonical Units of Analysis
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, ICLR 2025). Raw SAE latents are not canonical units (feature splitting/absorption). Motivates why 'build a more reliable unit' was the right question and why a single canonical multi-member answer should not be expected — backing the clustering-null finding.

sources.title: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders (Chanin et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, NeurIPS 2025). The SUPERVISED, spelling-bound absorption diagnostic (LR probe on first-letter labels → max-encoder-cosine parent; ablation on the first-letter logit + probe-projection). The absorption-reliability backdrop and the DELTA reference for CCRG's label-free recall-hole anchoring; David Chanin authored this and the reliability cites [4],[8].

sources.title: SAEs Are Good for Steering — If You Select the Right Features (Arad, Mueller, Belinkov)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, EMNLP 2025). SAEs become competitive with supervised steering only AFTER filtering to the right (specific, high-output-score) features; clean handles are a minority. Predicts the steering null and supports the concentration-not-absorption mechanism + localization-not-classification reframe.

sources.title: Can SAEs reveal and mitigate racial biases of LLMs in healthcare? (Ahsan & Wallace)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, ICLR 2026). Steers a single race-correlated SAE latent that CO-FIRES on the attribute and 'incarceration' (entanglement, not a clean absorber) and concedes SAE steering is 'of marginal utility for realistic tasks'. Corroborates the homograph-confined safety null — identity attributes are co-firing, not absorption-structured.

sources.title: Scaling Monosemanticity (Templeton et al., Anthropic 2024)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked). Specificity = 'when the feature is active, the relevant concept is reliably present'; safety-feature auditing framing. Grounds the safety-attribute-auditing use-case the screen serves and the concentrated-feature-as-sharp-gate intuition.

sources.title: Programming Refusal with Conditional Activation Steering / CAST (Lee et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, ICLR 2025 Spotlight). Gating a steering edit by a SUPERVISED condition vector fit on labeled example sets. The venue-backed anchor that the gating OPERATOR is established prior art — so the SAE-specific value can only be where the gate is discovered, the editing caveat in both forks.

sources.title: GSS: Gated Subspace Steering (Zhang, Shang, Li)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, preprint). Publishes essentially the exact gated-dense operator h'=h−G(|uᵀh|>ε)v with probe/steer OPTIMIZED on labeled sequences. Confirms the gated-dense control is prior art and supervised; the editing-is-not-a-novel-mechanism caveat.

sources.title: Beyond Interpretability: Label-Free Visual Steering (Chatzoudis et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked, preprint cs.CV). Trains a top-k SAE on unlabeled activations and steers without labels; establishes 'no labels needed' as a recognized value axis. Supports the label-free-discovery spine of both forks (modality note: vision).

sources.title: ICLR 2026 Call for Papers
sources.summary: Lists 'societal considerations including fairness, safety, privacy' and 'visualization or interpretation of learned representations' — clean area fit for the localization + feature-KG + label-free-screen framing. ICLR primary per goal; ICML fallback.

sources.title: Toy Models of Feature Absorption in SAEs (LessWrong)
sources.summary: CARRIED (locked). Defines absorption as 'gerrymandered SAE latents … with holes in their recall' — the recall-hole concept the localization procedure anchors on. Defensive novelty: using the recall hole as the selection anchor is the new step.

follow_up_questions: Which fork did M1'''' land — DEMONSTRATED_WHERE_TO_GATE_VALUE (and on the edit axis, the localization/gate-balanced-accuracy axis, or both) or FAIR_GATE_MATCHES_AT_N1? If FORK-A, what is the per-case break-even n_be (the FORK-A 'X' = smallest n at which the fair gate's CI overlaps the flat SAE handle)?

Did M3'''' return a single mined coverage number (fraction of N≥200–300 candidate polysemous tokens that are absorption-structured, with Wilson/bootstrap CIs and screen-vs-oracle agreement, stratified by hierarchy), or must S3.3 fall back to the locked breadth counts (2/44, 3/64, 0/28, months-only)?

Did the M5'''' stronger non-eval-aligned repair control (dense-probe argmax = parent; label-free non-recovery-aligned signal) + the downstream-capability test pay off (REPAIR_IS_NON_TAUTOLOGICAL_LOCALIZATION / DOWNSTREAM_CAPABILITY_FOUND), or must the presentation temper to 'the repair demonstrates correct LOCALIZATION, not utility'? Plus camera-ready cite resolution: Rauch et al. (2506.01992) author count/order; CorrSteer ICML-vs-ICLR-2026; SAEBench ICML-vs-ICLR; upgrade [2]/[3]/[8] if venues appear.
